jokes.text: You own his hair?
jokes.setup: Mrs. Reynolds claims Frank's toupee is hers
jokes.punchline: Mac's bewildered question about owning someone else's hair
jokes.explanation: Good economy and absurdist logic of 'owning' someone's fake hair

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You own his hair?

jokes.text: Divorce is a tricky thing. Oh! I found it. I got the hair now. Found the hair. So I guess I'll just, uh, see myself out.
jokes.setup: Mac awkwardly tries to console Mrs. Reynolds about her divorce
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to practical hair retrieval and escape
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward social interaction Mac would have

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Divorce is a tricky thing. Oh! I found it. I got the hair now. Found the hair. So I guess I'll just, uh, see myself out.

jokes.text: Right. Thank you for the hair.
jokes.setup: Awkward exchange about divorce and toupee retrieval
jokes.punchline: Treating hair handoff like any normal transaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and economy - 'thank you for the hair' is inherently absurd

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Reynolds
dialogue.line: Right. Thank you for the hair.

jokes.text: Oh, shit.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Reynolds' seductive behavior becomes apparent
jokes.punchline: Mac's dawning realization of the situation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat that sets up the main plot

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, shit.

jokes.text: Wild sex. Whoa! I mean... You name it, we did it.
jokes.setup: Frank describing his wild past with Angie
jokes.punchline: Catching himself oversharing but then continuing anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Frank's oversharing and lack of filter

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Wild sex. Whoa! I mean... You name it, we did it.

jokes.text: Why? Why would a person wipe their own shit onto a wall?
jokes.setup: Dee discovers vandalism in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Her existential confusion about the logistics of such behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Sunny gross-out humor with philosophical bewilderment

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Why? Why would a person wipe their own shit onto a wall?

jokes.text: What? Cleaning toilet? No, that's what Charlie does. I'm not doing Charlie work anymore.
jokes.setup: Dee tells Mac to clean the toilet
jokes.punchline: Mac's dismissive attitude and introduction of 'Charlie work' concept
jokes.explanation: 'Charlie work' becomes a running gag and perfectly captures the gang's hierarchy

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What? Cleaning toilet? No, that's what Charlie does. I'm not doing Charlie work anymore.

jokes.text: I guess it doesn't, really.
jokes.setup: Dee asks how Mac's plan helps her
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual admission that his plan is entirely selfish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac selfishness - acknowledges it doesn't help Dee but doesn't care

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I guess it doesn't, really.

jokes.text: Are you not wearing makeup? Really? Do you have any more?
jokes.setup: Dennis inspecting his date's appearance
jokes.punchline: His blunt criticism and request for more makeup
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis narcissism and inability to be socially appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Are you not wearing makeup? Really? Do you have any more?

jokes.text: I'm gonna bang her tonight, probably around 10:30 or so. Now, I really don't wanna do that... so all you have to do to stop me is call my cell phone... by 10:30... and say, 'Dennis, you don't have to do Charlie work anymore.'
jokes.setup: Dennis blackmailing Charlie with sexual threat
jokes.punchline: The calculated precision and casual delivery of his horrible plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Dennis moment - treating sexual blackmail like a business transaction

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm gonna bang her tonight, probably around 10:30 or so. Now, I really don't wanna do that... so all you have to do to stop me is call my cell phone... by 10:30... and say, 'Dennis, you don't have to do Charlie work anymore.'

jokes.text: Dennis's mom tried to have sex with me. Yeah, man, she got naked, she came on to me... That woman is straight crazy, but I think I wanna bang her.
jokes.setup: Mac confessing his dilemma to Charlie
jokes.punchline: Admitting he wants to sleep with his friend's mother despite knowing it's wrong
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's central plot with Mac's moral conflict

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dennis's mom tried to have sex with me. Yeah, man, she got naked, she came on to me... That woman is straight crazy, but I think I wanna bang her.

jokes.text: An opportunity like this only comes around once in a lifetime, right? You'd be a fool to let it slip through your fingers.
jokes.setup: Mac asking for advice about sleeping with Dennis's mom
jokes.punchline: Charlie treating it like a legitimate business opportunity
jokes.explanation: Charlie's twisted logic presented as sage wisdom

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: An opportunity like this only comes around once in a lifetime, right? You'd be a fool to let it slip through your fingers.

jokes.text: I am not having sex with you, Charlie.
jokes.setup: Charlie mentions wanting something from Dee
jokes.punchline: Dee's immediate assumption and rejection
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Dee knows Charlie well enough to preemptively reject him

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I am not having sex with you, Charlie.

jokes.text: It's not sex I want from you. It's sex I don't want from Dennis.
jokes.setup: Dee assumes Charlie wants sex
jokes.punchline: Charlie's convoluted explanation of his actual scheme
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay and misdirection with perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It's not sex I want from you. It's sex I don't want from Dennis.

jokes.text: Well, I've slowed down quite a bit once I met my husband, Carl. Oh, you told me you weren't married. Oh, I'm not. Carl passed a few years ago.
jokes.setup: Frank expecting wild stories from his old flame
jokes.punchline: Angie revealing she's a widowed mother instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection subverting Frank's expectations

dialogue.speaker: Angie
dialogue.line: Well, I've slowed down quite a bit once I met my husband, Carl.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, you told me you weren't married.

dialogue.speaker: Angie
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm not. Carl passed a few years ago.

jokes.text: For starters, you've got kids and shit. And you talk about 'em. You talk about your kids, you talk about your dead husband. I mean, it's kind of bummin' me out.
jokes.setup: Angie sharing about her family life
jokes.punchline: Frank's brutally honest and selfish reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank selfishness - complaining about normal human experiences

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: For starters, you've got kids and shit. And you talk about 'em. You talk about your kids, you talk about your dead husband. I mean, it's kind of bummin' me out.

jokes.text: Frank, I'm two years younger than you. That's what I'm talkin' about.
jokes.setup: Frank saying Angie is too old for him
jokes.punchline: The illogical age math and Frank's confidence in his position
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic and perfect Frank delusion with great economy

dialogue.speaker: Angie
dialogue.line: Frank, I'm two years younger than you. That's what I'm talkin' about.

jokes.text: I... love you.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to get invited inside
jokes.punchline: His desperate manipulation tactic deployed way too early
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis manipulation - dropping 'I love you' as a tactical move

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I... love you.

jokes.text: Looks like we got a blown fuse in here. Better get on that. Blown fuses count as Charlie work.
jokes.setup: Charlie avoiding his blackmail deal with Dennis
jokes.punchline: Finding a technical excuse to maintain the status quo
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the Charlie work running gag with Charlie's scheming

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Looks like we got a blown fuse in here. Better get on that. Blown fuses count as Charlie work.

jokes.text: That was, like, the best sex I've ever had, Mrs. Reynolds. Like something magical happened.
jokes.setup: Mac post-sex with Dennis's mother
jokes.punchline: His earnest romantic feelings about a revenge hookup
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac naivety - treating a purely physical encounter as magical

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That was, like, the best sex I've ever had, Mrs. Reynolds. Like something magical happened.

jokes.text: You're not gonna start talking about your feelings and shit, are you? No. Gay.
jokes.setup: Mac expressing romantic feelings
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Reynolds' blunt rejection and casual homophobia
jokes.explanation: Shows Mrs. Reynolds' terrible personality matching her son's

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Reynolds
dialogue.line: You're not gonna start talking about your feelings and shit, are you? No. Gay.

jokes.text: It doesn't 'un-bang' your mom. You gotta do something worse... You're gonna have to bang Mac's mom.
jokes.setup: Dennis wanting violent revenge on Mac
jokes.punchline: Charlie's logical escalation to sexual revenge
jokes.explanation: 'Un-bang' is perfect invented terminology, and the escalation logic is brilliantly twisted

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It doesn't 'un-bang' your mom. You gotta do something worse... You're gonna have to bang Mac's mom.

jokes.text: I think you're an ugly man. You think I'm ugly? I'm not ugly. You're ugly.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to seduce Mac's mother
jokes.punchline: Her blunt rejection and Dennis's childish response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis ego destruction - reduced to 'I'm not ugly, you're ugly'

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Mac
dialogue.line: I think you're an ugly man.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You think I'm ugly? I'm not ugly. You're ugly.

jokes.text: That move always works with the ladies. Frank, you have never gotten a chick with that move. Really? Yeah. So don't go blamin' the move. The move works.
jokes.setup: Frank's terrible dancing being rejected
jokes.punchline: His delusional confidence despite empirical evidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank delusion - insisting his obviously terrible move works

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That move always works with the ladies.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Frank, you have never gotten a chick with that move.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah. So don't go blamin' the move.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The move works.

jokes.text: Charlie is forcing me to have sex with him. Mac's getting laid. You're getting laid. Now even Charlie's getting laid? This makes me look terrible.
jokes.setup: Dee explaining Charlie's sexual blackmail
jokes.punchline: Her concern being about her own sexual reputation rather than coercion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee narcissism - missing the point of sexual coercion entirely

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Charlie is forcing me to have sex with him. Mac's getting laid. You're getting laid. Now even Charlie's getting laid? This makes me look terrible.

jokes.text: Am I the only one around here who's not getting laid?
jokes.setup: Dennis realizes everyone else is having sex
jokes.punchline: His narcissistic outrage at being left out
jokes.explanation: Dennis making everything about himself. Good character moment showing his priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Am I the only one around here who's not getting laid?

jokes.text: I'm gonna stab his face off. No, stabbing's bad. You need to do something worse... You gotta sleep with Charlie.
jokes.setup: Waitress wanting violent revenge on Dennis
jokes.punchline: Charlie escalating to sexual revenge again
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the revenge escalation pattern, though less impactful than the first

dialogue.speaker: The Waitress
dialogue.line: I'm gonna stab his face off. No, stabbing's bad. You need to do something worse... You gotta sleep with Charlie.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, sweetie. I just don't find you sexually attractive.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to seduce Charlie's mother
jokes.punchline: Another brutal rejection of Dennis's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of Dennis's sexual failures, though less original

dialogue.speaker: Charlie's Mom
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, sweetie. I just don't find you sexually attractive.

jokes.text: Are you kidding me? You don't find me sexually attractive? What in the hell is going on? What is wrong with you people?
jokes.setup: Dennis being rejected by another woman
jokes.punchline: His incredulous breakdown at facing repeated rejection
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissistic worldview completely shattered by reality - his confusion is genuine

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Are you kidding me? You don't find me sexually attractive? What in the hell is going on? What is wrong with you people?

jokes.text: I only slept with you to piss Frank off. Afterward I felt like I was gonna throw up.
jokes.setup: Mac hoping for a relationship with Mrs. Reynolds
jokes.punchline: Her brutal honesty about using him for revenge
jokes.explanation: Effective dark humor showing how everyone uses everyone else

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Reynolds
dialogue.line: I only slept with you to piss Frank off. Afterward I felt like I was gonna throw up.

jokes.text: Check this out. L...
jokes.setup: Dennis starting to say 'I love you' to Mac
jokes.punchline: The implication of his manipulation attempt
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Dennis's manipulation pattern

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Check this out. L...

jokes.text: Hey, guys, uh, can you do me a favor and not get too drunk? All right? 'Cause I got some kegs that I need changing. There's, like... There's some rats down in the rattraps.
jokes.setup: Dennis supposedly free from Charlie work
jokes.punchline: Immediately doing Charlie work himself
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - Dennis ends up doing exactly what he tried to avoid

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Hey, guys, uh, can you do me a favor and not get too drunk? All right? 'Cause I got some kegs that I need changing. There's, like... There's some rats down in the rattraps.

jokes.text: I had sex with your father... because, just like you, I like my sex old and ugly. And with fake hair on their heads that falls off when you're having sex with 'em.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Reynolds confronting Dennis about sleeping with Frank
jokes.punchline: Her graphic description of Dennis's sexual preferences
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation with callback to the toupee from the opening

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Reynolds
dialogue.line: I had sex with your father... because, just like you, I like my sex old and ugly. And with fake hair on their heads that falls off when you're having sex with 'em.

jokes.text: And with fake hair on their heads that falls off when you're having sex with 'em. It just falls off because that's what you do, Dennis Reynolds.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Reynolds describing her revenge sex with Frank
jokes.punchline: The toupee falling off during sex brings the hair subplot full circle
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that ties the toupee subplot into the sexual revenge plot

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Reynolds
dialogue.line: And with fake hair on their heads that falls off when you're having sex with 'em. It just falls off because that's what you do, Dennis Reynolds.

jokes.text: You said, 'I love you,' and so I thought, 'Okay, I love you too.' How do you show love? You go and have sex with old people. So that's what I did too!
jokes.setup: Dennis's manipulation with 'I love you'
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Reynolds' twisted logic about expressing love through revenge sex
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation tying together Dennis's manipulation with twisted relationship logic

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Reynolds
dialogue.line: You said, 'I love you,' and so I thought, 'Okay, I love you too.' How do you show love? You go and have sex with old people. So that's what I did too!
